title: Processing Your Sadness
description: A Windows-style dialog sincerely attempting to process your emotional state. It never finishes.
idea: A fake system dialog box that is earnestly trying to defragment, compress, and optimize your sadness like it's a hard drive. Progress bars inch forward, tasks complete one by one (Locating Childhood Wound... Patching self-worth.dll...), and just when it's about to complete, an error occurs and it must start over. Infinite, sincere, and deeply unsettling.

plan.title: Processing Your Sadness
effects: Progress timer that advances and completes tasks
interactions: OK button on error that restarts
plan.visual_style: Windows 98/XP era dialog box aesthetic, centered on a dark desktop background
plan.implementation_notes: Use CSS to simulate old Windows dialog chrome. Animate progress bar with setInterval. Each task takes varying time. Error at ~95% completion.

title: Singing Shadows
description: You walk into a room where your reflection hums a lullaby only it can hear, and it whispers answers that may or may not be yours.
idea: A minimalist, single-page page showing a flickering shadow that speaks in reversed sentences. Tapping a shadow reveals fragmented memories or absurd truths. The interaction feels like peeking into a broken television that speaks back in your own voice, challenging what you believe about identity and silence.

plan.title: Singing Shadows
state_variables: flickerState: boolean to control shadow's flickering (true/false)
key_interactions: Tap shadow to reveal new fragmented memory/absurd truth
plan.visual_style: Dark gradient background (#0a0a2f) with semi-transparent shadow (#5555ff at 30% opacity) that flickers. Whispers appear in pale silver text (#cce6ff) with reversed word order.
plan.key_implementation_notes: Uses setInterval for flicker effect via inline opacity changes. Touch events handle mobile compatibility. Whisper phrases are predefined array sliced/reversed on tap.useCallback optimizes tap handler performance.
